summary: Completed E2E testing of the enhanced Diary feature with DiaryDetailModal. All backend watch entry CRUD APIs (22/22 tests passed) and frontend modal functionality working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/user/watch-history creates entry with watches array
Backend: POST /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches adds a new watch entry
Backend: POST /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches accepts 0.1 increment ratings
Backend: POST /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches validates rating 0-10
Backend: POST /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches uses default current date
Backend: POST /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches returns 404 for non-existent movie
Backend: POST /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches truncates comment to 500 chars
Backend: PUT /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} updates rating
Backend: PUT /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} updates date
Backend: PUT /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} updates comment
Backend: PUT /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} updates all fields
Backend: PUT /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} returns 404 for invalid watch_id
Backend: PUT /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} returns 404 for invalid movie
Backend: DELETE /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} removes watch but keeps movie
Backend: DELETE /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} removes movie when last watch deleted
Backend: DELETE /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} returns 404 for invalid watch
Backend: DELETE /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id} returns 404 for invalid movie
Backend: GET /api/user/watch-history returns watches array with auto-migration
Backend: Summary fields (user_rating, last_watched_date, watch_count) sync with watches
Backend: Add/Update/Delete watch entries require authentication (401 without token)
Frontend: Clicking diary item opens DiaryDetailModal
Frontend: Modal shows movie poster, title, watch count, latest rating
Frontend: Watches displayed in descending order (latest first)
Frontend: 'Log a New Watch' button opens form with rating slider, date picker, comment textarea
Frontend: Edit button on watch entry opens inline edit form with rating, date, comment
Frontend: Delete button removes watch; if last watch, modal closes and diary updates
Frontend: Watch labels show 'First Watch' for earliest and 'Re-watch #N' for subsequent
Frontend: My Movies page all 3 tabs working (Diary, Watchlist, Profile)
Frontend: Toast notifications shown for watch added/updated/removed/movie removed
critical_code_review_comments: All required data-testids implemented correctly: diary-detail-modal, diary-detail-title, diary-detail-close, add-watch-btn, new-watch-rating, new-watch-date, new-watch-comment, confirm-add-watch-btn, watches-list, watch-entry-{id}, edit-watch-{id}, delete-watch-{id}, edit-rating-{id}, edit-date-{id}, edit-comment-{id}, save-edit-{id}
Backend _sync_watch_summary helper correctly updates user_rating, last_watched_date, watch_dates, watch_count from watches array
Auto-migration logic in GET /api/user/watch-history correctly creates watches array from legacy watch_dates
Delete last watch correctly removes movie from history and returns {removed: true}
test_credentials: Unique test users created per test run with timestamp-based emails
seed_data_creation: None - tests create own data dynamically
context_for_next_testing_agent: Diary Detail Modal fully tested. Key data-testids: diary-detail-modal, diary-detail-title, diary-detail-close, add-watch-btn, new-watch-rating, new-watch-date, new-watch-comment, confirm-add-watch-btn, watches-list, watch-entry-{id}, edit-watch-{id}, delete-watch-{id}. Watch entry endpoints: POST /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches, PUT /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id}, DELETE /api/user/watch-history/{tmdb_id}/watches/{watch_id}. JWT auth required - token stored as 'chef_token' in localStorage. Watch labels show 'First Watch' for chronologically earliest and 'Re-watch #N' for subsequent watches. Watches displayed in descending order (latest first). Deleting last watch removes movie from diary entirely.
